Business Plan

1. Executive Summary

GreyhoundIQ is a modern, mobile-first Australian greyhound racing data platform that aggregates national race data into a single, beautifully designed interface. It replaces the fragmented, outdated, and overpriced tools that currently serve this market.

The Opportunity

The Australian greyhound racing industry turns over $4.3 billion in annual wagering across ~500,000 active punters. Yet the data tools serving this audience are stuck in 2010 — user-contributed, ad-heavy, GBP-priced, and fragmented across 6+ state body websites. Nobody offers AI predictions, unified national data, or a decent mobile experience.

$4.3B
Annual AU greyhound wagering
500K
Active punters (est.)
$99/yr
Pro tier (21% below competitor)
~$14/mo
Infrastructure cost

Win on Features

AI race predictions, DNA genetic matching, GPS tracking, unified national data, 5-gen pedigrees, and speed maps — features no competitor offers.

Win on Design

Mobile-first, ad-free, modern UI. Every current player has weak visual design. A polished product stands out immediately.

Win on Price

AUD pricing (not GBP), cheaper Pro tier than the competitor's top plan, and a more generous free tier as a lead magnet.

GreyhoundIQ at a glance — the market, the projections, and why now.
Business Plan

Business Plan

3. Competitive Landscape

The current market is served by a global database player (greyhound-data.com), a News Corp–owned specialist publisher (The Greyhound Recorder), state body sites (The Dogs, GRV), and general racing portals. All have significant weaknesses — and none combines national data, AI, and a habit-forming product. A full competitor deep-dive follows the matrix below.

The problem & the opportunity — how GreyhoundIQ compares across every feature that matters.
One platform. All states. Real-time data. Smarter tools. Better price.

Competitor Matrix

Competitor Type Strengths Weaknesses Pricing
greyhound-data.com Main rival Largest breeding DB globally. International coverage. User-contributed data. GBP pricing for AU users. Outdated PHP/JQuery UI. Ad-heavy. User-contributed = unreliable. No GPS/AI. Poor mobile. £17–£65/yr
~AUD $33–$125
thedogs.com.au (GRNSW) State body Official NSW data. Free. Live streaming. NSW only. 403-blocks all bots. Basic data depth. No breeding tools. Free
FastTrack / GRV State body Isolynx GPS data. Litter/stud search. VIC only. Track stats free. VIC only. Form guides behind login. Dated government UI. Free (login required)
OzChase State body NSW participant portal. Throwing server errors. Legacy ASP.NET. Unreliable. Free (participants)
Punters.com.au Portal Form guides. Speed maps. Multi-code coverage. Not greyhound-specialised. CloudFront-blocked. Shallow data depth. Freemium
Betfair Hub Data/Dev Free CSVs. "Iggy" ML model. Historical data back to 2020. Developer-focused, not consumer. Betting-market data only, no form/breeding. Free
Tasracing FormPlus State body Clean modern UI. Form, replays, Black Book. TAS only. Small market. Freemium (PRO tier)

Positioning Map

Competitor deep-dive: The Greyhound Recorder

Beyond greyhound-data.com, the most formidable Australian competitor is The Greyhound Recorder — a specialist title founded in 1931, acquired by News Corp's Punters.com.au in 2019, and run today inside the News Perform / Punters / Odds ecosystem. A detailed review of its public surface shows a deep editorial-plus-data product that monetises through wagering affiliate links rather than subscriptions — and, critically, leaves the most valuable ground wide open.

~126K
Monthly visits (May 2026, est.)
71%
Australian audience
75
Track-result directories (AU + NZ)
$0
Subscription revenue — affiliate-led

Where it's strong

  • Deep AU/NZ track coverage; free printable form guides (Fields / Short / Long form)
  • Rich entity pages — greyhound profiles with form, pedigree, progeny, litter tabs
  • Stud directory with fees, breeder contacts, PDFs — a genuine niche asset
  • Trainer & track profiles; high editorial cadence; strong direct-traffic loyalty

Where it's weak (our opening)

  • No user accounts, watchlists, or black book — nothing to bring users back
  • No replay library, live video, or post-race race-intelligence layer
  • No mobile app; no public API; template QA failures ("Race undefined")
  • Mixed intent — half product, half bookmaker funnel; no AI anywhere

Greyhound / pedigree profiles✓✓✓✓✓ + AI
AI race predictions✕✕✕✕✓ ML
DNA / genetic matching✕✕✕basic✓ Testmate + COI
Watchlist / black book✕✓✓forum✓ planned
Replay / race intelligence✕✓✓ Isolynxvideo DB✓ + pace maps

Mobile app / PWA✕?✓ Watchdog✕✓ PWA
Public API✕✕✕✕✓ Pro+
Ad-free / AUD pricingad-led✓✓GBP✓ AUD

Five gaps → five GreyhoundIQ advantages

The competitor review's conclusion was blunt: the incumbents win on coverage but lose on habit. None has built the return-loop features. Each gap is a GreyhoundIQ opportunity, mapped to where it lands in the product.

1 · Personalisation & black book

No incumbent lets you follow dogs, sires, trainers, or tracks with alerts. GreyhoundIQ ships watchlists + race-day/result/scratch alerts — the single biggest habit-former. Free + Pro

2 · Rich media & race intelligence

Combine previews with replays, pace maps, sectional analysis, and AI probability overlays — the "watch + analyse + return" loop the Recorder lacks entirely. Pro+

3 · Mobile productisation

Racing is mobile and time-sensitive. A fast PWA with cards, watchlists, alerts, and replay clips beats a print-oriented website outright. All tiers

4 · QA & data confidence

The Recorder ships visible "Race undefined" template failures. For a product whose value is trust in data, being visibly cleaner and more consistent wins serious users. Core

5 · Welfare & non-punter journeys

Incumbents are wagering-adjacent. Pairing race intelligence with transparent welfare, ownership, and education journeys broadens audience legitimacy — and SEO reach. Brand

The counter-position

Less publisher, more platform. The incumbents are read-only reference sites. GreyhoundIQ is a daily-habit platform — accounts, AI, alerts, community — built on the same data they have, plus the intelligence they don't.

Why this matters commercially

The Greyhound Recorder proves the audience is real and loyal (~126K monthly visits, ~17-minute sessions, 71% Australian) — but monetises only through affiliate click-outs, leaving subscription revenue entirely on the table. GreyhoundIQ targets exactly that gap: convert loyal research behaviour into paid, habit-forming product use. The incumbent has validated demand without capturing its value.

Business Plan

5. AI Race Intelligence

GreyhoundIQ's machine-learning engine is the single biggest differentiator in the market. No competitor offers race predictions, and none can match a purpose-built model trained on five-plus years of unified national form, sectional, and GPS data.

The Intelligence Moat

Every other player shows you the data and leaves you to interpret it. GreyhoundIQ interprets it for you — turning raw form into a ranked, probability-weighted prediction with a transparent confidence score. This is the feature punters will pay for, and the one competitors can't copy without the data and the model.

ML Win Probability

A gradient-boosted model ranks every runner with a calibrated win probability and confidence interval — trained on box, distance, track, going, trainer, and sectional history.

AI Speed Maps

Predicted early-speed and settling positions for every box, modelled from career sectionals — so punters can see how a race will be run before it's run.

Model Confidence

Every prediction ships with a transparent confidence score (e.g. 89.7%). Punters know when the model is sure and when a race is genuinely open.

Performance Forecasting

Projected times and finishing positions per runner, benchmarked against the track record and class par — surfacing value the market hasn't priced.

Key-Factor Explainability

Each pick is broken down into the factors that drove it — late speed, box draw, track suitability — so the model is a tool punters trust, not a black box.

Prediction API

Pro+ subscribers and partners can pull predictions programmatically — powering tipster tools, betting calculators, and the wider racing-data ecosystem.

The AI Predictions surface — ranked runners, the factors behind each pick, and live model confidence.
5+ yrs
Training data (Betfair + national form)
~90%
Target model confidence on strong races
0
Competitors offering AI predictions
Pro+
Tier where full AI unlocks
Business Plan

6. DNA & Genetic Matching

For breeders and owners, GreyhoundIQ turns pedigree guesswork into genetic science. The DNA & Genetic Matching suite combines five-generation pedigrees, inbreeding analysis, and trait prediction into a single matchmaking workflow — the breeding tool the industry has never had.

Stop breeding on hunches. Breed on the genetics.

Testmate Matchmaking

Cross any sire with any dam in the database. GreyhoundIQ runs 1,000 simulated matings and projects winners, placings, fast-time rate, and an earnings index for the litter.

Inbreeding Coefficient (COI)

An automatic COI gauge for every proposed pairing — colour-coded green (<5%), amber (5–10%), red (>10%) — so breeders avoid the inbreeding that quietly erodes performance and health.

Genetic-Risk Flags

Flags carrier-risk for known recessive conditions across the proposed cross — a genetic-health safeguard that protects litters, buyers, and the breed.

Mendelian Trait Prediction

Models the likely inheritance of performance and conformation traits from both bloodlines, so breeders can target the qualities they're breeding for.

5-Generation Pedigrees

Interactive pedigree trees with win-rate, stakes and track records at every node — the bloodline at a glance, back five generations.

"Top Dams to Pair" AI

Given a sire, GreyhoundIQ ranks the best dams to pair with — and vice versa — turning the whole database into an active matchmaking engine.

Testmate — model a litter from any sire × dam, with the inbreeding-coefficient gauge and genetic-risk flags.
Why this is an add-on, not a checkbox

Genetic matching is a distinct, high-value job for a distinct, high-willingness-to-pay audience — breeders and stud farms. Packaging it as a DNA Match add-on (bundled into Pro+, or available as a stand-alone upgrade on Pro) creates a second revenue line beyond the punter-focused tiers, and positions GreyhoundIQ as the data backbone of Australian greyhound breeding.

1,200
Active AU breeders (target market)
1,000
Simulated matings per Testmate run
5-gen

9. Operational Plan

Lean operation — one developer (AI-assisted), automated data pipelines, near-zero infrastructure management.

Data Pipeline

Automated Python workers run on cron. Betfair CSVs seed 5 years of history. TAB API provides daily live race fields. FastTrack (VIC) and Tasracing scraped for stats/breeding. Community contributions fill gaps.

Infrastructure

Single Hetzner VPS (4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, ~$8/mo). Next.js app + PostgreSQL + Redis all on one box. Migrate to managed Postgres only when concurrent users exceed ~500. Cloudflare in front for CDN/DDoS protection.

Team

Solo developer (Daniel) using Hermes Agent + AI-assisted development for 10x output. No hires needed until Year 2. Then: 1 part-time data engineer for pipeline maintenance, 1 part-time content writer for SEO.

Monitoring & Quality

Automated health checks on ingestion pipeline. Data accuracy dashboard comparing our results against official sources. User feedback/report system for data discrepancies. Sentry for error tracking.

Data Sources & Reliability

SourceData TypeCostReliabilitySetup Time
Betfair CSVs5yr history, BSP, Iggy modelFreeHighImmediate
TAB APILive race fields, all statesFreeMedium1 week
FastTrack (GRV)VIC stats, breeding, stewardsFreeHigh1 week
Tasracing FormPlusTAS form, results, replaysFreeHigh3 days
Betfair Exchange APIReal-time odds, streamingFree (acct)High1 week
GRNSW PartnershipNSW fields, results, form$$ TBDHigh4–8 weeks
GRV PartnershipIsolynx GPS data$$ TBDHigh4–8 weeks
Business Plan

10. Data Sources & Feed Strategy

GreyhoundIQ is only as good as its data. This section scopes every feed the platform depends on — what it provides, how we access it, what it costs, and the legal basis for using it. The guiding principle: build on official, licensed data wherever possible, with public-data fallbacks to de-risk launch.

The key strategic finding

There is no neutral, openly-published national greyhound data API (unlike thoroughbred racing's Racing Australia feed). Greyhound racing is regulated state-by-state. In practice, one system has become the de-facto national spine: the Topaz API, operated by Greyhound Racing Victoria, covering all 7 states + NZ with history back to 2020, and distributed through Betfair Australia. GreyhoundIQ's data architecture anchors on Topaz, supplemented by Betfair market data and the Greyhounds Australasia stud book, with public sources as fallback.

Primary feeds — fully scoped

SourceProvidesAccess & AuthCostLegal basisRole
Topaz API (GRV) Form, fields, results, runners — all 7 states + NZ; history to 2020 REST/JSON, official Python wrapper. API key via Betfair ANZ (automation@betfair.com.au) Negotiated No public price; gated via Betfair acct Licence required Commercial redistribution needs GRV agreement Primary spine
Betfair Hub CSVs Historical market/BSP files (2020→), "Iggy" greyhound model outputs Direct CSV/ZIP download — no login Free Open Community/educational, AU/NZ History seed
Betfair Exchange API Live + historical odds, traded volume, BSP (no form) REST/JSON-RPC + Stream. App key + login ~£499 live key (delayed key free) Dev T&Cs Vendor licence to redistribute live Live odds
Greyhounds Australasia Stud Book National breeding/pedigree, DNA/parentage, naming Downloadable PDF volumes (parse). Portal login for DNA Free (recent volumes) © GA Bulk redistribution likely restricted Pedigree
Isolynx (GRV) GPS tracking / sectional analytics (VIC tracks) Currently free CSV/JSON; moving to "personal-use" API TBD Restricted Not official timing; commercial licence unconfirmed GPS (Pro+)

Fallback & supplementary feeds

SourceProvidesAccessWhy fallback only
TAB (unofficial JSON)Live fields, scratchings, fixed + tote odds, dividends — all statesOpen/undocumented endpoint; AU IP requiredPersonal-use ToS Fields are state-body copyright; not a commercial basis
thedogs.com.au (GRNSW)Fields, PDF formguides, results, stewards — all statesNo API; Cloudflare-fronted site"Any process" ban Explicit no-reproduction-without-consent clause
Tasracing FormPlusTAS form, sectionals, replaysWeb only; no APIUse Topaz (TAS) Cleaner licensed route exists
OzChaseNoms, grading, lifecycle/integrity (NSW/QLD/SA)Participant login; fee-based public request via RQAdmin DB Not an open feed
Compliance: race-fields licensing is a hard cost line

Even where data is technically obtainable, commercial redistribution of race fields to bettors triggers state "Race Fields Information Use" approvals and fees. NSW (GRNSW) charges a $2,500 + GST application fee under the Betting and Racing Act 1998; other states run analogous product-fee regimes. This is budgeted as a distinct compliance line item, not an afterthought — and it is the single biggest gating factor between "scraped MVP" and "licensed commercial platform."

Phased data strategy

Phase 1 — Seed & validate

Free Betfair Hub CSVs seed 5+ years of history and BSP. Public sources validate coverage. Build the canonical schema. No commercial redistribution yet — private beta only.

Phase 2 — License the spine

Open the Betfair ANZ relationship, obtain the Topaz API key, and negotiate GRV commercial redistribution terms. File NSW race-fields approval. This is the gate to public launch.

Phase 3 — Deepen & differentiate

Layer Isolynx GPS (as licensing allows), GA stud-book pedigree parsing, and live Betfair odds. Pursue direct GRV/GRNSW partnerships for richer, faster, fully-licensed feeds.

Why multi-source is the moat, not the risk

No single feed gives GreyhoundIQ everything — and that's the point. Form comes from Topaz, odds from Betfair, pedigree from GA, GPS from Isolynx. Stitching these into one normalized national dataset is exactly the work competitors haven't done. The redundancy also de-risks the business: if one source changes terms, the others keep the platform running.

Business Plan

11. Risk Analysis & Mitigation

Key Strengths

  • Near-zero infrastructure cost (~$14/mo) means profitability from first paying customer
  • Multiple free data sources confirmed — no single-source dependency
  • Competitor is entrenched in outdated tech and unlikely to modernise quickly
  • 97% gross margin — subscription model with zero marginal cost per user
  • AI predictions and GPS data are moats that competitors can't easily replicate
  • Australian-specific focus builds local trust and community

Key Risks

  • Data partnerships could be expensive or denied — mitigated by free scraping fallbacks
  • TAB API or scraped sites could change structure — mitigated by multi-source strategy
  • Racing authorities may object to scraping — mitigated by only using publicly accessible data and pivoting to partnerships
  • greyhound-data.com could modernise in response — mitigated by AI features they can't replicate and AU-specific focus
  • Responsible gambling compliance requirements — must implement 18+ checks and Gamble Responsibly messaging
  • Seasonal user behaviour — racing is year-round but wagering peaks spring carnival

Risk Heat Map

Marketing Plan

12. Target Audience

Three primary personas drive GreyhoundIQ's marketing strategy. Each has distinct needs, channels, and willingness to pay.

"The Serious Punter" — Primary (60% of revenue)

Male, 35–65 · Bets $200–$2,000/week · Values data over tips

Bets on greyhounds 3–5 days a week. Currently uses a mix of free sites, Punters.com.au, and maybe a greyhound-data.com subscription. Frustrated by fragmented data, outdated UIs, and the lack of predictions. Wants speed maps, sectional times, box bias stats, and AI predictions to find an edge. Will pay $12–$29/month for a genuine advantage. This is the core Pro and Pro+ subscriber.

Primary target Pro / Pro+ tier LTV: $300–$600/yr

"The Breeder/Trainer" — Secondary (25% of revenue)

Mixed demographics · Industry participant · Values pedigree data

Active in the breeding/training side of the industry. Needs pedigree databases, testmating tools, sire statistics, litter performance tracking. Currently relies on greyhound-data.com (unreliable, user-contributed) and FastTrack (VIC only). Will pay for accurate, national breeding data with modern tools. This persona values depth and accuracy over speed/predictions.

Secondary target Pro tier LTV: $99–$249/yr

"The Casual Punter" — Tertiary (15% of revenue, 70% of users)

Broad demographics · Bets occasionally · Values simplicity

Bets on greyhounds occasionally — maybe during big races or when at the pub. Checks form on their phone before a bet. Currently uses free sites or just bets on vibes. Won't pay for data but will use the free tier and convert to Pro if they get serious. This persona is the top of the funnel — they drive SEO traffic and free-to-paid conversion.

Funnel driver Free → Pro conversion LTV: $0 → $99/yr
Marketing Plan

13. Brand Positioning

Positioning Statement

For Australian greyhound racing enthusiasts who want a genuine edge, GreyhoundIQ is the data platform that combines official national race data, AI predictions, and breeding analytics in a modern, ad-free interface — unlike greyhound-data.com, which is outdated, unreliable, and charges in foreign currency.

Brand Personality

Authoritative but accessible. Data-driven, not stuffy. The "smart friend" who knows greyhound racing inside out — not the old-school bookie type.

Key Differentiators

1) Only platform with AI predictions. 2) Only unified national data source. 3) AUD pricing. 4) Modern mobile-first design. 5) API access for developers.

Tone of Voice

Direct, confident, data-backed. No hype, no false promises. "Here's what the numbers say." Australian, not corporate. Responsible gambling messaging woven throughout.

Brand Pillars

Authority

Official data, not user-contributed

Intelligence

AI predictions nobody else offers

Simplicity

Clean, fast, mobile-first UX

Value

More features, less money, AUD

Marketing Plan

Marketing Plan

15. Content & SEO Strategy

SEO is the primary growth engine. Racing data is highly searchable — every dog name, every track, every sire is a keyword. Build pages for all of them.

Programmatic SEO

Every dog in the database gets a SEO-optimised profile page (e.g. /dogs/aston-queen). Every track gets a stats page (e.g. /tracks/wentworth-park/box-stats). Every sire gets a progeny page. This creates 10,000+ indexable pages targeting long-tail keywords with high intent.

Content Hub

Publish 2–3 articles/week targeting informational keywords: "best greyhound racing tips", "how to read greyhound form", "Wentworth Park box bias guide", "top sires in Australia 2026". Each article links back to relevant data pages, driving conversion to Pro.

Target Keywords (Sample)

KeywordMonthly Volume (AU)DifficultyIntentTarget Page
greyhound form guide8,100MediumTransactional/races
greyhound racing results6,600LowInformational/results
greyhound racing tips5,400MediumTransactionalContent article
wentworth park results1,900LowInformational/tracks/wentworth-park
greyhound breeding1,300LowInformational/breeding
barcia bale progeny320Very LowInformational/sires/barcia-bale
greyhound race cards today2,400LowTransactional/ (homepage)
[dog name] form50–200 eachVery LowInformational/dogs/[name]
SEO Math

With 10,000+ programmatic pages averaging just 5 visits/month each = 50,000 monthly organic visits. At 8% free signup rate = 4,000 new signups/month. At 22% Pro conversion = 880 new Pro subscribers/month. This is the growth engine.

Marketing Plan

16. Go-to-Market & Launch Plan

A staged launch that builds momentum from community seeding to public launch.

Stage 1 — Pre-Launch (Weeks 10–12)
Community Seeding
Identify and engage with the greyhound racing community on Reddit (r/AusGreyhounds, r/greyhoundracing), Twitter racing community, and greyhound racing forums. Share useful data insights. Build relationships before asking for anything. Get early feedback on the product from 20–30 community members.
Reddit Twitter Beta testers
Stage 2 — Soft Launch (Week 13)
Free Tier Public
Launch the free tier publicly. Announce on Reddit, Twitter, and racing forums. Focus message on "free, modern, national greyhound racing data — no ads, no GBP pricing." Collect email addresses for Pro tier waitlist. Publish first 5 SEO content articles.
Free tier live Email waitlist SEO content starts
Stage 3 — Pro Launch (Week 15)
Paid Tiers Go Live
Email the waitlist with a launch offer (e.g. 50% off first 3 months). Enable Stripe billing for Pro and Pro+ tiers. Publish comparison content ("GreyhoundIQ vs greyhound-data.com"). Start YouTube channel with race analysis videos using the platform.
Pro billing live Launch offer YouTube channel
Stage 4 — Scale (Week 16+)
Growth Engine
Ramp up content production to 3 articles/week. Launch weekly email newsletter with race previews. Partner with 2–3 tipsters/podcasters for co-marketing. Begin testing Google Ads on high-intent keywords. Start programmatic SEO page generation at scale.
3 articles/wk Newsletter Partnerships Paid ads test
Marketing Plan

17. Partnership Strategy

Tipster Partnerships

Partner with greyhound racing tipsters on Twitter and YouTube. Offer them free Pro+ accounts in exchange for promoting GreyhoundIQ to their audience. Revenue share model: 20% recurring commission for any subscriber they refer.

Podcast Sponsorships

Sponsor 2–3 Australian racing podcasts (e.g. racing industry podcasts). Negotiate promo code deals — listeners get 20% off, podcast gets revenue share. Target: ~5,000 downloads/episode podcasts.

App/Tool Integrations

Offer API access to other racing tools, betting calculators, and tipster platforms. Pro+ API tier creates a B2B revenue stream and positions GreyhoundIQ as the data backbone for the ecosystem.

Industry Body Relationships

Build relationships with GRV, GRNSW, and Racing Queensland. Position GreyhoundIQ as a partner that grows the sport by improving the punter experience. Explore co-marketing opportunities and official data licensing.

Marketing Plan

Marketing Plan

19. Marketing Budget

Lean startup approach — reinvest revenue into marketing rather than upfront spend.

Year 1 Marketing Budget Allocation ($15,000 total)
Budget Philosophy

Year 1 marketing budget is $15,000 — funded entirely from Year 1 revenue (~$104K). The majority (53%) goes to content and YouTube, which compound over time. Paid ads are deliberately small until organic channels prove out. The budget scales proportionally with revenue: Year 2 targets $50K marketing spend, Year 3 $100K.

GreyhoundIQ — Business & Marketing Plan · Prepared by AI Kick Start · 28 June 2026
